chapter_title: Printed books, newspapers, pictures and other products of the printing industry; manuscripts, typescripts and plans
scope_note: Note 2: 'printed' includes reproduced by duplicating machine, ADP machine, embossed, photographed, photocopied, thermocopied or typewritten. Note 3: newspapers/journals bound otherwise than in paper, or sets comprising >1 number under single cover → 49.01. Note 4: 49.01 also covers pictorial reproductions with text (numbered pages for binding), pictorial supplements to bound volumes, and printed parts of books in assembled/separate sheets. Note 5: publications ESSENTIALLY devoted to advertising → 49.11 (not 49.01).

inclusions.description: Printed books, brochures, leaflets and similar printed matter, whether or not in single sheets. Includes: collections of art reproductions with text (numbered for binding); pictorial supplements to bound volumes; printed book parts (assembled/separate signatures). Note 3: news/journals bound non-paper or multi-issue under single cover → 49.01. Note 5 EXCEPTION: essentially advertising publications → 49.11
Newspapers, journals and periodicals, whether or not illustrated or containing advertising material (printed at least four times a week — regular frequency publications)
Children's picture books and picture drawing or colouring books. Note 6: 'children's picture books' = books where PICTURES form the PRINCIPAL INTEREST and text is SUBSIDIARY
Music, printed or in manuscript, whether or not bound or illustrated
Maps and hydrographical or similar charts of all kinds, including atlases, wall maps, topographical plans and globes, printed

Plans and drawings for architectural, engineering, industrial, commercial, topographical or similar purposes, being originals drawn by hand; hand-written texts; photographic reproductions on sensitised paper and carbon copies of the foregoing

Unused postage, revenue or similar stamps of current or new issue in the country in which they have, or will have, a recognised face value; stamp-impressed paper; banknotes; cheque forms; stock, share or bond certificates and similar documents of title
Printed or illustrated postcards; printed cards bearing personal greetings, messages or announcements, whether or not illustrated, with or without envelopes or trimmings
Calendars of any kind, printed, including calendar blocks

Other printed matter, including printed pictures and photographs: advertising material (brochures, leaflets, trade catalogues, tourist propaganda — per Note 5); printed pictures/illustrations without text (whether in signatures or separate sheets — per Note 4 last paragraph); photographs commercially printed

exclusions.condition: Photographic negatives or positives on transparent bases (photographic film)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 37 (photographic or cinematographic goods) — Note 1(a). Exposed/unexposed photographic film → Ch.37

exclusions.condition: Maps, plans or globes IN RELIEF (three-dimensional raised surface), whether or not printed
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 90.23 (instruments, apparatus and models, for demonstrational purposes) — Note 1(b). 3D relief maps and globes → 90.23. Flat printed maps → 49.05

exclusions.condition: Playing cards or other goods of Chapter 95 (toys, games, sports requisites)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 95 — Note 1(c). Playing cards (tarot, poker cards) → 95.04. Board game cards/pieces → 95.04. Puzzle books that are primarily toys → Ch.95

exclusions.condition: Original engravings, lithographs, postage/revenue stamps (used), first-day covers, antiques >100 years
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 97 — Note 1(d). Original artistic prints/lithographs (hand-pulled, limited edition) → 97.02. Used stamps and philatelic items → 97.04. Antique books/prints >100 years → 97.06. UNUSED stamps of current issue → 49.07

exclusions.condition: Publications essentially devoted to advertising (brochures, pamphlets, leaflets, trade catalogues, tourist propaganda)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 49.11 — Note 5. Key test: is the PRIMARY PURPOSE advertising? If yes → 49.11. Books that happen to contain some advertising → 49.01. Exception: newspapers/journals containing advertising → stay in 49.02 per Note 3

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — 'Printed' has broad definition: includes ADP output, photocopies, thermocopies, embossed
classification_rules.description: For Ch.49, 'printed' is not limited to conventional printing presses. It explicitly includes: duplicating machines (stencil/spirit duplicators), AUTOMATIC DATA PROCESSING machine output (laser, inkjet printer output is 'printed'), embossed text, photographed pages, photocopied documents, thermocopied faxes, and typewritten text. A laser-printed report → 'printed'. A hand-written manuscript → NOT printed (→ 49.06). A photocopy of a document → 'printed' for Ch.49 purposes.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 49.01 (books) vs 49.11 (advertising/other printed matter): the 'essentially devoted to advertising' test
classification_rules.description: The critical distinction between 49.01 (books/brochures with informational/educational purpose) and 49.11 (advertising matter): NOTE 5 states that publications ESSENTIALLY DEVOTED TO ADVERTISING → 49.11. Test: what is the primary purpose? INFORMATIONAL/EDUCATIONAL primary purpose → 49.01 (even if it contains some ads). PRIMARY PURPOSE = promoting sales of goods/services → 49.11. Examples: trade catalogue = 49.11; cookery book by food brand (primarily recipes) = 49.01; company annual report = borderline (likely 49.11 if promotional). Exception: newspapers/journals (49.02) may contain advertising without being redirected.

classification_rules.rule: Note 5+Note 4 — Picture books vs illustrated books vs pictures without text
classification_rules.description: Three heading outcomes for picture/illustration content: (1) 49.01 = books where TEXT is principal and pictures are illustrative; OR pictorial works with text on numbered pages suitable for binding. (2) 49.03 = CHILDREN'S PICTURE BOOKS where PICTURES are the PRINCIPAL INTEREST and text is subsidiary (Note 6). (3) 49.11 = printed pictures/illustrations WITHOUT TEXT — whether in signature/separate sheet form. Key test: does the publication have meaningful text? Is it for children with pictures dominant? Or is it purely pictorial without text?

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — Newspapers in unusual binding still stay in 49.01 (not 49.02)
classification_rules.description: Heading 49.02 = regularly-issued newspapers, journals, periodicals (typically weekly or more frequent). When a periodical is BOUND IN NON-PAPER MATERIAL (leather, fabric, hardcover), or when MULTIPLE ISSUES are presented under a single cover → Note 3 directs these to 49.01 (books), not 49.02. A bound annual compendium of a newspaper's issues → 49.01. Regular single weekly issue of a magazine → 49.02. Collections of journals in permanent binding → 49.01.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 49.07 (unused stamps/banknotes/financial instruments) vs 97.04 (used stamps/philatelic) vs 49.11 (printed pictures)
classification_rules.description: Two categories of stamps: UNUSED STAMPS of CURRENT/NEW ISSUE with recognised face value in issuing country → 49.07 (valid currency/fiscal instruments). USED STAMPS, cancelled stamps, first-day covers, stamp collections, philatelic material → Chapter 97 (97.04). Reproduction prints of stamps for decorative purposes → possible 49.11 (printed matter). Banknotes in current circulation or newly issued → 49.07. Banknotes >100 years old → 97.06 (antiques). Commemorative medals → Ch.99 or Ch.71.
